title: Bitter Velvety Malaysian Tofu Broth
description: A soothing, bitter and velvety broth enriched with Malaysian flavors, perfect for a sick day.

ingredients.name: lemongrass
ingredients.notes: bruised and cut into 2 cm pieces

instructions: In a medium pot, bring the 1 liter of water to a gentle boil at 100°C.
Add the bruised lemongrass, sliced galangal, and torn kaffir lime leaves to the pot, and let simmer for 10 minutes to infuse the aromatics.
Stir in the sliced water chestnuts and let simmer for another 5 minutes.
Add the cubed tofu, palm sugar, low-sodium soy sauce, sumac, and white pepper to the broth.
Reduce heat to low and simmer gently for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld while keeping the broth calm.
Taste the broth; adjust seasoning with a pinch more soy sauce or palm sugar if needed.
Remove the pot from heat and let the broth rest for 5 minutes to deepen the bitterness.
Serve hot, pouring the broth into bowls and garnishing with sliced green onions.
Optional: squeeze fresh lime juice into each bowl for a bright contrast.
Enjoy immediately while the broth is still warm.
